description: REQ-020 EARS clause and CRIT-003 passThreshold are violated. The spec REQ-020 line 485 reads: 'WHILE AppShellState === \'Loading\' THE SYSTEM SHALL render only the global header shell (without full nav content) and a centered loading affordance.' CRIT-003 passThreshold reads: 'AppShell.svelte source renders <header> in Loading state (skeleton/empty nav) and Configured state'. The implementation in AppShell.svelte:55-65 wraps <header> in `{#if state === "Configured"}` only — Loading state has NO header. The Loading branch at AppShell.svelte:72-92 renders a <main> with a skeleton inside, but no <header>. Concretely, when bootOrchestrator is pending, the user sees a skeleton inside <main> with no header chrome, which is the inverse of what REQ-020 mandates. This is a direct contradiction between behavior and approved CRIT — the rework that this sprint exists to deliver was not executed. Routes to Phase 2b for re-implementation.
